title: How to automatically create mini-deadlines based on final deadline?
excerpt: I want to create a TEMPLATE where if I assign a final deadline to a project, it will automatically assign mini-deadlines to various tasks that need to be completed first. For example, Task A (writing a script) is due fiv…

posts.author: cedricft (Cedric H)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to create a TEMPLATE where if I assign a final deadline to a project, it will automatically assign mini-deadlines to various tasks that need to be completed first. For example, Task A (writing a script) is due five days before the final deadline, Task B (creating storyboards) is due three days before the final deadline, etc. I feel like this should be easy to do but for the life of me I can’t find a way to do it. Please help!

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @cedricft ,

Welcome to the monday community!

Since you mentioned a Template, I assume the process you are using is mostly if not completely repetitive, the only thing changing being the deadlines.

I would recommend two things.

First, create a dependency column for theses tasks and do the necessary connections.

Support

Dependencies on monday.com

8 min read Feature     Do you have items on your board with due dates that are dependent on one another? Can one item not start until the other is complete? That's where dependencies comes in...

Second, once you have set up the original board, you can make it a Template board.

Support

monday.com board templates

6 min read Feature     There are a number of pre-prepared and fully customizable templates that you can choose from whenever you create a new board in your account. Whether you're looking fo...

Hope this helps!

Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com
